subject: [PATCH v5 0/5] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not address the issue of pool_mode being hardcoded to pernode, and requested further changes to ensure that other modes are properly removed.
reviews.raw_body:
Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/5] sunrpc: route to a populated pool in svc_pool_for_cpu()
      commit: abf002b5412b282ce825cf0ada22f0278ae7f408
[2/5] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes
      commit: cb4ab2c71e09f8c958ecc7bf07270ae78584f85a
[3/5] sunrpc: guarantee a thread per pool when auto-distributing
      commit: c972bef38717f27c282592bca22890f49233ec0a
[4/5] sunrpc: tear down pool counters before dropping the pool map reference
      commit: 9b6f69571d09627b8b4303be0f257c0e36d9d3fd
[5/5] sunrpc: derive the pool count instead of caching it in sv_nrpools
      commit: 9435623ac560654825a62ee4b628ae4bbaa87920

--
Chuck Lever

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed a concern about exporting svc_pool_map, explaining it's currently private to svc.c and thus cannot be exported.

Author acknowledged a minor improvement to the code, agreed to send a follow-up patch to remove an unnecessary if statement, and thanked the reviewer.
reviews.raw_body:
That would mean that we would have to export svc_pool_map, which is
currently private to svc.c.

---

Ahh good point. I guess we can remove that if statement. I can send
Chuck a follow-on patch. Thanks for the R-b!

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch series hardcodes the pool mode to per-node, removing other modes and addressing issues such as scheduling RPCs to pools with no threads. It also implements suggestions from Neil to eliminate the serv.sv_nrpools field, which has the side effect of reducing the modulus operations in the svc_pool_for_cpu() hotpath. The patches include fixes for comment cleanups, shortcoming of existing code, and auto-thread-placement case. The approach taken is to ensure that each populated node has at least one thread and prevent routing requests to empty pools.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the if() condition in svc_pool_for_cpu() will never be true because all values stored in svc_pool_map.to_pool are less than svc_pool_map.npools, but this does not affect the correctness of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
The values stored in svc_pool_map.to_pool are all less than
svc_pool_map.npools.
So that if() condition cannot be true. 

But those two things don't reduce the correctness of the patch.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks for doing this.
NeilBrown

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch series hardcodes the pool mode to per-node, removing other modes and addressing issues such as scheduling RPCs to pools with no threads. It also implements suggestions from Neil to eliminate the serv.sv_nrpools field, which has the side effect of reducing the modulus operations in the svc_pool_for_cpu() hotpath. The patches include fixes for comment cleanups, shortcoming of existing code, and auto-thread-placement case. The approach taken is to ensure that each populated node has at least one thread and prevent routing requests to empty pools.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown questioned why svc_pool_map is more problematic to export than svc_serv_nrpools, suggesting inlining the function or marking it inline and exporting it
reviews.raw_body:
Why is exporting svc_pool_map more problematic than exporting svc_serv_nrpools?
I think it would be good for at least the code in svc_pool_for_cpu() to
inline the function.  Maybe it already does?  Or maybe marking it
"inline" but still exporting it would work.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed NeilBrown's suggestion to make the svc_pool_map structure accessible from nfsd.ko, explaining that exposing internal details is preferable and would require making the definition public.
reviews.raw_body:
Doing what you suggest would increase the interface "surface" between
sunrpc.ko and nfsd.ko.

Currently struct svc_pool_map is only defined in net/sunrpc/svc.c, so
we'd not only need to export the symbol, but also we'd have to make
that definition public.

Why expose so many internal details to other modules when all we need
is this one accessor? I think keeping this interface small is
preferable.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a valid concern about the hot path in svc_pool_for_cpu() and agrees to revisit the idea if it can be demonstrated that it matters, but does not have time for it currently.
reviews.raw_body:
Your point is certainly valid, and this _is_ a hot path. If we can
demonstrate that this matters, then I'd be happy to revisit the idea. I
don't have the cycles to do so at the moment though.

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch series hardcodes the pool mode to per-node, removing other modes and addressing issues such as scheduling RPCs to pools with no threads. It also implements suggestions from Neil to eliminate the serv.sv_nrpools field, which has the side effect of reducing the modulus operations in the svc_pool_for_cpu() hotpath. The patches include fixes for comment cleanups, shortcoming of existing code, and auto-thread-placement case. The approach taken is to ensure that each populated node has at least one thread and prevent routing requests to empty pools.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown noted that hardcoding pool_mode to pernode may have costs, such as increased code size, and requested further consideration of these trade-offs.
reviews.raw_body:
In general, yes.  But it is not without cost.  Sometimes the cost
matters.
Interface size has an abstract value.  Code size can have concrete
value.  Comparing them is subjective I guess.
I have no concrete evidence that there is a problem, so I'll stop
objecting.

Thanks,
NeilBrown
